# dokploy_backup (Resource)

A scheduled backup of a Dokploy database or application. Stores artefacts in a `dokploy_destination`.

**Limitation:** `database_type = "web-server"` (application backups) is not supported in this provider version because `application.one` does not return a `backups[]` field, making the post-create ID discovery impossible.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Backup of a managed Postgres database.
# Supported database_type values: postgres, mysql, mariadb, mongo.
# (web-server backups are not supported in this provider version because the
# Dokploy API does not list them on application.one.)
resource "dokploy_backup" "db_daily" {
  database_type  = "postgres"
  database_id    = dokploy_postgres.db.id
  destination_id = dokploy_destination.s3.id
  schedule       = "0 3 * * *"
  prefix         = "postgres/app/"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `database_id` (String) Identifier of the resource being backed up (`dokploy_postgres.x.id`, `dokploy_application.x.id`, etc).
- `database_type` (String) What kind of resource is being backed up. One of `postgres`, `mysql`, `mariadb`, `mongo`, `web-server`.
- `destination_id` (String) `dokploy_destination.x.id`.
- `prefix` (String) Path prefix inside the bucket where backup files are written.
- `schedule` (String) Cron expression (ex: `0 3 * * *`).

### Optional

- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the schedule is active. Defaults to `true`.
- `keep_latest_count` (Number) Retention — keep only the latest N backups. Omit to keep all.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import dokploy_backup.db_daily <backupId>
```
